item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would assume Dr. Reuel's middle name is Ronald?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The new image is compliant with CC BY-SA 3.0.

Tags have been updated to reflect this.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Source: https://pixabay.com/images/id-403685/
License: CC0 (See Note)

Title: N/A
Author: RegioTV
Uploaded on: 2014/07/30

Note: While Pixabay has adapted Pixabay License since January of 2019, they also has clarified that any images uploaded before the new license went into effect remains under CC0. Please see here for details.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it originates from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, too many things that try to be funny will begin to become just either references or random shenanigans, but this is like almost a normal comedy skit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic. A skip that plays with the comedic/absurd side of the world, but doesn't fall into self-parody.

Also, I think I just somehow managed to upvote this twice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey hey, you posted it.

You've largely fixed all the issues I had with the piece during the review phase, I quite like the final product. Sir Gregor's so overly dramatic and the fact that he can't remember the riddle now, after all this time has a charming irony to it.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it before and I still love it now. Great work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, so I decided to re-include a small point that the sediment making up the tunnels appear to be indestructible, hope that'll help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this a couple times as a draft on IRC, and I liked it then.

Solid +1, well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I figure the fact an anomalous entity exists within the tunnels and apparently only because of the tunnels they would designate it a number

I didn't get that from the article. At most, it's implied that some kind of anomalous connection exists between the tunnels and the knight, but it's problematic because it isn't made explicit in the documentation. In-universe, it looks as if the Foundation considers the tunnels to be an anomaly almost just because -1 says something 'powerful' exists within, among other things.

I did mention that the interview is in fact translated from Middle English

Oh, right. I see that now. Guess my brain just blocked that out for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've actually thought about that.

I figure the fact an anomalous entity exists within the tunnels and apparently only because of the tunnels they would designate it a number.

As for the interview, I'll certainly see what I can do to tighten it up.

However, I did mention that the interview is in fact translated from Middle English. The doctor they got to talk with Sir Gregor is from the Linguistic Division, this is meant to say he knows Middle English.

Thanks for the feedback

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two issues I'm seeing here. Firstly, what exactly makes the network of tunnels anomalous? Right now, they're described but there's nothing that explicitly explains why they've been given an SCP designation in the first place. Secondly, I feel that the interview could stand to be tightened up a little, in terms of cutting the initial introductions/expositing to get straight to the riddle shenanigans. I'm not a fan of using interviews as an expository, so I initially got a little bored before the riddle part came up. Oh, also, the guy speaks modern English, so that breaks disbelief somewhat, though admittedly I'm not sure how 15th century English actually sounded like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heyooooo I remember seeing this in the forums! Hearty +1 from me. Glad to see more of this sort of writing on the mainlist!

item.title: "The Sphinx that forgot its riddle" as someone else put it
item.content:encoded: Thanks to anybody and everybody that helped with the draft, there were a lot, specially you Storm!
